mnemonics.title: FSTAB FIELDS ORDER
mnemonics.content: THE ORDER OF FSTAB FIELDS CAN BE REMEMBERED AS: DEVICE MOUNTS TWICE (DMTFO) - Device, Mount point, Type, File system options, dump, fsck order. ALTERNATIVE MNEMONIC: DADS (Device, Always type, Dump, Sequence).

mnemonics.title: MOUNT OPTIONS SECURITY
mnemonics.content: SECURITY MOUNT OPTIONS (COMMON ONES): 'NOSUID NOEXEC NODEV' - Think of IT AS 'SUDDENLY NO DEVICE EXECUTION' OR REMEMBER: 'SENSIBLE NODES ENFORCE ZERO EXECUTION'. THESE PREVENT SU, EXEC, AND DEVICE FILES FROM BEING USED.

mnemonics.title: MOUNT VS UMOUNT
mnemonics.content: TO MOUNT: SPECIFY TYPE, DEVICE, AND DIRECTORY. TO UNMOUNT: SPECIFY EITHER THE DEVICE (/DEV/SDA1) OR THE MOUNT POINT (/MOUNT/USB). REMEMBER: YOU 'MOUNT' A DEVICE TO A 'POINT', AND 'UNMOUNT' EITHER ONE.

acronyms.usage: Configuration file in /etc that defines which file systems to mount automatically at boot time with their options and parameters.
Kernel abstraction layer that provides a uniform interface for different file system types (ext4, NTFS, NFS, etc.).
Distributed file system protocol that allows mounting remote directories over a network.
Unique identifier assigned to file systems; preferred over device names in /etc/fstab for consistent mounting across reboots.
Linux kernel module and user-space daemon that provides automatic mounting of file systems on demand.

visualTips.visualization: IMAGINE AN INVERTED TREE WITH THE ROOT (/) AT THE TOP. BRANCHES ARE DIRECTORIES. WHEN YOU MOUNT A DEVICE AT /MEDIA/USB, YOU ESSENTIALLY HANG ANOTHER MINI-TREE FROM THAT BRANCH. THE ORIGINAL DIRECTORY CONTENTS BECOME HIDDEN UNTIL UNMOUNT.

THINK OF MOUNTING LIKE A MARRIAGE CEREMONY: THE FILE SYSTEM 'MARRIES' A DIRECTORY. THEY BECOME ONE. AFTER THE CEREMONY (MOUNT), YOU ACCESS THE SPOUSE THROUGH THE WIFE OR HUSBAND NAME. TO SEPARATE (UNMOUNT), THEY MUST AGREE AND NO ONE CAN BE USING EITHER SPOUSE.

A MOUNT POINT IS LIKE A DOORWAY TO ANOTHER ROOM (FILE SYSTEM). WHEN YOU MOUNT AT /HOME/USER/USB, YOU'RE PUTTING A DOOR AT THAT LOCATION. WALK THROUGH IT AND YOU'RE IN THE USB'S WORLD. UNMOUNT CLOSES THE DOOR AND HIDES THE ROOM.

analogies.analogy: IT'S LIKE OPENING A NEW BOOK AND PLACING IT ON YOUR DESK. YOUR DESK IS THE MAIN DIRECTORY TREE. THE USB IS A SEPARATE BOOK. MOUNTING PUTS THE BOOK ON YOUR DESK SO YOU CAN READ ITS PAGES (FILES). UNMOUNTING IS PUTTING THE BOOK BACK ON THE SHELF.

IMAGINE HAVING THE SAME PHOTO IN TWO DIFFERENT FRAMES ON YOUR WALL. BOTH FRAMES SHOW THE SAME PICTURE, BUT IT'S ONE PHYSICAL PHOTO. CHANGES IN ONE FRAME IMMEDIATELY APPEAR IN THE OTHER BECAUSE THEY POINT TO THE SAME ORIGINAL.

THINK OF LAZY UNMOUNT AS PUTTING A 'FOR RENT' SIGN ON AN APARTMENT BUILDING. YOU ANNOUNCE YOU'RE MOVING OUT (DETACH), BUT YOU WAIT UNTIL ALL TENANTS (PROCESSES) LEAVE BEFORE ACTUALLY CLEANING UP (CLEANING UP RESOURCES).
